data.text: I found existing tests and partial Linux process-group handling, so I’m checking whether the remaining gap is the startup reaper and the docker backend path rather than the basic Pdeathsig wiring. Next I’m reading the executor, backend, and tests in full to see what already exists versus what still needs implementation.

input.command: /bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '250,360p' cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go && printf '\\n---\\n' && sed -n '660,730p' cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go && printf '\\n---TESTS---\\n' && sed -n '1,260p' cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend_unix_test.go && printf '\\n---EXE TESTS---\\n' && sed -n '1,240p' cli/internal/agent/executor_test.go"

data.text: The Linux executor already has `Pdeathsig` and `LockOSThread`, so the likely missing piece is the orphan reaper and a stronger test around actual parent-death behavior. I’m tracing where `ddx work` starts and where an orphan scan can run without touching unrelated workers.

input.command: /bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' cli/internal/agent/runner.go && printf '\\n---attempt backend top---\\n' && sed -n '1,220p' cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go"
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' cli/cmd/root.go && printf '\\n---WORK---\\n' && rg -n \"func .*Work|ddx work|work command|claim next bead|Bead\" cli/cmd cli/internal | sed -n '1,240p'"

data.text: The worktree may already contain partial fixes, so I’m checking the exact diff before I touch anything. If the missing behavior is already present in uncommitted edits, I’ll narrow to the remaining gap rather than duplicating changes.

input.command: /bin/zsh -lc 'git diff -- cli/internal/agent/executor.go cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go cli/internal/agent/executor_windows.go cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go cli/internal/agent/executor_test.go cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend_unix_test.go cli/cmd/root.go'

data.text: The tree is clean, and the Linux `Pdeathsig` work is already committed in the base snapshot. I’m now looking for the actual orphan-reaping hook and its tests, because that is the only acceptance item that still appears plausibly unimplemented.

If the targeted tests and pre-commit are green, I’ll decide whether the correct end state is a no-op evidence file or a tiny evidence commit, depending on what the tree needs.
